hypothesis: Training single-step retrosynthesis models with a class-balanced objective—using inverse-square-root template-frequency weighting plus oversampling of templates with ≤10 training examples—will increase top-10 accuracy on test reactions whose templates have training frequency ≤10 by ≥20 percentage points for LocalRetro and MEGAN and ≥10 percentage points for RootAligned, and will increase Pistachio OOD top-10 accuracy by at least fivefold, relative to identical models trained without balancing.
